---
title: 🍎Progress Presentation🍎
subtitle: "<br/> Pennsylvania Public Middle and High School Lunch Menus"
author: 
  - "Olivia Ngai"
  - "Josie Kelley"


output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css:  "default"
    nature:
      slideNumberFormat: "%current%"
      highlightStyle: solarized-light
      highlightLines: true
      ratio: 16:9
      countIncrementalSlides: true
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
options(htmltools.dir.version = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```

```{css, echo=FALSE}

.remark-slide-content {
  background-color: #8FBC8F;
  font-family: 'American Typewriter', serif;
}

.remark-slide-content h1 {
  color: #FFFAFA;
  font-size: 3rem;
  padding: 0.5rem 1rem;
  margin: -1rem -4rem 1rem -4rem;  /* ← pulls edges out */
  background-color: #2F4F4F;
  border: 3px solid #8B4513;
}

/* This overrides the above just for the title slide */
.title-slide h1 {
  background-color: transparent;
  color: #2F4F4F;
  border: none;
  font-size: 3rem;
  margin: 0;
  padding: 0;
}


.remark-slide-content h2 {
  color: #8B4513;
  font-size: 1.7rem;
}

/* Title slide */
.title-slide {
  background-color: #8FBC8F;
  color: #ffffff;
}
.title-slide h1 { color: #2F4F4F; border-bottom: none; font-size: 3rem; }
.title-slide h2 { color: #CADCFC; font-size: 3.5rem; }
.title-slide.author { color: #8B0000; font-size: 1.1rem; }


/* Accent / highlight boxes */
.highlight-box {
  background-color: #FFF8DC;
  color: #2F4F4F;
  border-radius: 8px;
  font-size: 1.8rem;
  padding: 0.8rem 1.2rem;
  margin: 0.5rem 0;
}

.text-box {
  background-color: #ADD8E6;
  color: #000000;
  font-size: 1.6rem;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 0.8rem 1.2rem;
  margin: 0.5rem 0;
}
.center-box {
  background-color: #FFF8DC;
  color: #2F4F4F;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 0.8rem 1.2rem;
  margin: 0.5rem 0;
  font-size: 2rem;
  top: 70%;
  text-align: center;
}
.chalkboard {
  color: #FFFAFA;
  background-color: #2F4F4F;
  border: 3px solid #8B4513;
}
.footer {
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 1rem;
  left: 1rem;
  font-size: 1rem;
  font-family: "Google", sans-serif;
  color: #2F4F4F;
}
.remark-code {
  font-size: 0.8rem;  /* ← shrink this number to make it smaller */
}

/* Two-column layout helpers */
.left-col  { float: left;  width: 48%; }
.right-col { float: right; width: 48%; }
.clear     { clear: both; }


```


# Research Question
.center-box[
"To what extent does district-level income influence the diversity and nutritional content of middle and high school lunch menus in Pennsylvania?"
]
.left-col[
.text-box[
__Importance__
- School lunches account for up to 50% of a child's daily caloric intake
- Lower income students are more reliant on school lunches
- Informs policy gaps and setting goals for future policy
]
]
.right-col[
## Key Variables
.chalkboard[
- 🏫 School district 
- 💰 District-level income 
- 🥗 Food diversity
- 🧮 Nutritional score 
]
]
???
- middle and high schools in PA
- average household income at the district level
- diversity of meal options offered throughout the month
  - Do meals vary in terms of category?
  - Do food items repeat daily, weekly, monthly?
- will be calculated from fat, sodium, sugar, fiber, and protein
---

# Tractable School Lunch Data: Nutrislice 

.left-col[
## Challenges
.text-box[
- No uniform reporting of lunch menus 
  - PDFs
  - School websites
  - Third party platforms
  - Gaps in publicly accessible lunch data
  - Incomplete nutrition statistics across districts
]
]

.right-col[
## Benefits of Nutrislice
.text-box[
- 1/4 of PA school districts use Nutrislice (98)
- Standardized data format across all districts
- Accessible nutritional information
- Consistent menu reporting structure (JSON)
]
]

---
# Data Retrieval Step 1: Selenium
.left-col[
## Finding Schools on Nutrislice
.text-box[

- Obtained PA school district list from the PA Department of Education
- Used Selenium to loop through each school district on Nutrislice
- Filtered results for middle and high schools only
]
]
.right-col[
.remark-code[
```{r eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
driver = webdriver.Chrome()
driver.get("https://lookup.nutrislice.com/en/")
wait = WebDriverWait(driver, 10)

all_results = []
schools_nutri = []
for school in school_districts:
    # Locating search box
    search_box = driver.find_element(By.ID, "org-input")
    time.sleep(2)
    # Clear last search
    search_box.clear()
    search_box.send_keys(school)
    search_box.send_keys(Keys.RETURN)

    time.sleep(1)
    
    options = driver.find_elements(By.CSS_SELECTOR, "mat-option")
    for opt in options:
        #Save all results to a list
        all_results.append(opt.text)
        schools_nutri.append(school)
driver.quit() 
```
]
]
---
# Data Retrieval Step 2: Creating API links
.left-col[
## Formulating API links
.text-box[
- Identified a repeatable URL pattern across all Nutrislice schools
- Automated API link construction by extracting the domain and school name from each URL
  - The highlighted text was retrieve from the original link
]]
.right-col[
.remark-code[
```{r eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
# Getting school link domains using links list
for link in links:
    domains = [link[8:link.index(".")] for link in links]

#Getting school name from the link
for link in links:
    schools  = 
    [link.split("menu/")[1].split("/lunch")[0] for link in links]

#Building API link using original link, 
#school name, and domain name
api_links=[]
count=-1
for domain in domains:
    count+=1
    api_link = ("https://" + domain + 
    ".api.nutrislice.com/menu/api/weeks/school/" +
     schools[count] + "/menu-type/lunch/2026/03/01/")
    api_links.append(api_link)
    
```
]]

.footer[
## Example

https://<mark>tmsd</mark>.nutrislice.com/menu/<mark>tussey-mountain-high</mark>/lunch/2026-03-18

https://<mark>tmsd</mark>.api.nutrislice.com/menu/api/weeks/school/<mark>tussey-mountain-high</mark>/menu-type/lunch/2026/03/01/

]

---
# Step 2 continued: Daily Items API
## Some school menu items were offered daily. These items were in separate APIs.
.left-col[
.remark-code[
```{r eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
api_links_messages=[]
for link in message_urls:
    #the new API link using lists of these elements
    link = link + "/messages/school/" + str(
    school_ids[message_urls.index(link)]) + "/menu-type/" + 
    str(menu_ids[message_urls.index(link)]) +"/"
    api_links_messages.append(link)
```
]
.text-box[
- Identified the pattern for formulating this API link
- Required a menu id from another API and the school name
- Automated API link construction
]]
.right-col[
<img src="images/daily_menu.png" style= "width:100%;">
]
.footer[
## Example
  - https://<mark>bedfordasd</mark>.api.nutrislice.com/menu/api/messages/school/<mark>7049</mark>/menu-type/<mark>8719</mark>/
]
---

# Data Retrieval Step 3: Scraping Menu Items
## Built two scraping functions to handle different JSON structures
.left-col[
.text-box[
 __Main_menu()__
  - Looped over 5 weekly API requests per school
]
.remark-code[
```{r eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
    date_map = {
        'week_1': "01",
        'week_2': "08",
        'week_3': "15",
        'week_4': "22",
        'week_5': "29” }
        
```
]
.text-box[
__Daily_menu()__
- Instead of a date put "daily"
]
]
.right-col[
.remark-code[
```{r eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
all_rows.append({
  "school_name": school_name,
  "menu_id": menu_id,
  "date": date,
  "food_id": food.get("id"),
  "food_name": food.get("name"),
  "category": category,
  "ingredients": food.get("ingredients"),
  "cal": nutrition.get("calories"),
  "g_fat": nutrition.get("g_fat"),
  "g_sat_fat": nutrition.get("g_saturated_fat"),
  "g_trans_fat": nutrition.get("g_trans_fat"),
  "mg_cholesterol": nutrition.get("mg_cholesterol"),
  "g_carbs": nutrition.get("g_carbs"),
  "g_added_sugar": nutrition.get("g_added_sugar"),
  "g_sugar": nutrition.get("g_sugar"),
  "mg_potassium": nutrition.get("mg_potassium"),
  "mg_sodium": nutrition.get("mg_sodium"),
  "g_fiber": nutrition.get("g_fiber"),
  "g_protein": nutrition.get("g_protein"),
  "mg_iron": nutrition.get("mg_iron"),
  "mg_calcium": nutrition.get("mg_calcium"),
  "mg_vitamin_c": nutrition.get("mg_vitamin_c"),
  "iu_vitamin_a": nutrition.get("iu_vitamin_a"),
  "mg_vitamin_d": nutrition.get("mg_vitamin_d"),
  "serving_size_amount": serving_size_info.get("serving_size_amount"),
  "serving_size_unit": serving_size_info.get("serving_size_unit"),
  })
```
]]
---
# Preliminary EDA: Exploring Food Categories
.left-col[
## Filtered for unique food items per school
__Entrees__ have the highest count
- Median is around 41 with a max near 80 (averaging around 1-2 entrees per day)
- Schools differ greatly on the number of entrees they offer 

__Vegetables and fruit__ are the next most common 
  - Veggies median is around 15 (likely repeat throughout the month)
  - Fruit median is around 10 (likely repeat throughout the month)
- Condiments, grain, meat, milk, sides, and snacks have medians near zero
]
.right-col[
<img src="images/category_eda.png" style= "width:100%;">
## Restraints and Improvement
- Lower limit of all categories is zero
- Indicates some schools might not completely categorize their food items
- Inconsistent data
]
---

# Next steps:  Feature engineering and Supervised learning

.left-col[
__Supervised machine learning for Categorizing__
- Train a classification model on existing categorized food items 
- Predict and fill in missing categories based on food item names

__Calculating Nutrition Score__
- Python package Nutriscore
- positive weights for fiber and protein
- negative weights for sodium, saturated fats, and added sugars



__Incorporating more Data for linear regression__
- District-level mean income (Based on tax returns)
- Geographic variables (Urban vs. Rural)
- Percentage of students qualifying for free and reduced lunches (School specific)
]

.right-col[
## Nutrition (Per School)
.chalkboard[
- 🥗 Score each food item using NutriScore
- 🥗 Average the score per day
- 🥗 Average score per month
- 🥗 Category specific scores
- 🥗 <mark>Nutrition score for each school</mark>
]

## Food Diversity (Per School)
.chalkboard[
- 🥗 Total unique items offered per month
- 🥗 Average unique items offered per day
- 🥗 Number of unique items in each category
- 🥗 Food diversity score
]
]
---
# Implications for Stakeholders

__Policy Makers__

- Provides evidence of income based nutrition disparities
- Can justify grant funding for low income districts
- Data driven findings can help allocate resources to districts with the greatest nutritional need

__School Administrators__

- More diverse menus could increase lunch participation and student wellbeing
- Low food variety may discourage students from eating school lunch entirely
- This project may discourage school administrators from using nutrislice if it exposes a failure to meet nutritional standards

__Public Health Officials__

- Poor nutrition in childhood is linked to lower academic performance and long term health outcomes
- Nutritional disparities in school lunches may reinforce existing socioeconomic inequalities

__Students and Families__

- Students dependent on school lunch are most vulnerable to poor nutrition and low food diversity
- Improved menu quality could directly impact short and long term health outcomes

---
# Ethical, Legal, and Societal Implications
__Ethical__

- Schools have a responsibility to provide nutritious options, particularly for students who rely on school lunch as their primary meal
- Poor nutritional offerings disproportionately impact low income students who have fewer alternatives
- Healthier menus are linked to better academic performance and long term health and well being

__Legal__

- Schools that fail to meet federal nutrition standards could face increased scrutiny or liability
- Districts with documented nutritional deficiencies may be subject to policy enforcement

__Societal__

- Nutritional disparities in school lunches risk widening existing socioeconomic inequalities
- Low income students who depend most on school lunch are most vulnerable to the consequences of poor menu quality
